title: Neobartsia peruviana
taxonomy: Neobartsia peruviana is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a type of parasitic plant, which means it obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The classification of Neobartsia peruviana is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Lamiales, and family Orobanchaceae. This plant is native to Peru and has a unique relationship with its host plants. The classification helps scientists understand the evolutionary history and relationships among different plant species.

intro: Neobartsia peruviana, also known as Peruvian angel's trumpet, is a flowering plant species in the family Orobanchaceae. Traditionally, Neobartsia peruviana has been used in folk medicine to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its reported anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Neobartsia peruviana include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds, such as aucubin and flavones, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including infusions and tinctures, to make a tea or supplement that is believed to promote immune function and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
